description: TSMC manufactures all of NVIDIA's advanced GPUs and AI accelerators on its cutting-edge process nodes
Direct competitors in GPU, AI accelerator, and data center markets
Competing in AI accelerators and data center GPUs; Intel is trying to enter the discrete GPU market
Broadcom provides networking chips and custom ASICs that complement NVIDIA's GPU platforms in data centers
Marvell supplies data infrastructure silicon used alongside NVIDIA GPUs in cloud data centers
Micron provides HBM (High Bandwidth Memory) used in NVIDIA's data center GPUs
Supermicro builds server systems featuring NVIDIA GPUs for AI data centers
Dell sells enterprise servers and workstations powered by NVIDIA GPUs
Broadcom supplies wireless connectivity chips and RF components for iPhones and other Apple devices
Qualcomm supplies 5G modem chips for iPhones, though Apple is developing its own modem
Apple is TSMC's largest customer; TSMC manufactures all of Apple's A-series and M-series chips
Compete in operating systems, cloud services, AI assistants, and productivity software
Compete in mobile operating systems (iOS vs Android), AI, and services
Google pays Apple billions annually to be the default search engine on Safari
Compete in smartphones, tablets, and consumer electronics globally
Samsung supplies OLED displays and memory chips for iPhones and other Apple devices
AWS and Azure are the two largest cloud providers, competing intensely for enterprise customers
AWS competes with Google Cloud; both compete in AI services and digital advertising
The two largest retailers in the US, competing across e-commerce and physical retail
Compete in cloud (Azure vs GCP), AI (OpenAI/Copilot vs Gemini), search, and productivity
Compete in enterprise software, databases, and cloud infrastructure
Oracle databases run on Azure; the companies collaborate on multi-cloud interoperability
Microsoft Dynamics 365 competes with Salesforce in CRM and enterprise applications
Compete in design, marketing, and document software; Adobe runs many workloads on Azure
The two dominant players in digital advertising, competing for ad dollars globally
Compete in social media and messaging; Meta has replicated Snapchat features across its apps
Meta is one of the largest buyers of NVIDIA GPUs for its AI research and recommendation systems
Google Cloud and DeepMind use NVIDIA GPUs extensively; Google also develops its own TPU chips
Compete in the electric vehicle market; Tesla leads in EVs while Ford rapidly expands its EV lineup
Compete in EVs and autonomous driving technology
Compete in the electric truck and SUV market
Palo Alto Networks provides cybersecurity solutions protecting Tesla's vehicle and factory networks
The two largest US banks by assets, competing across retail banking, investment banking, and wealth management
Compete in investment banking, trading, and asset management
Compete in retail banking, mortgages, and consumer lending
The two dominant global payment networks, processing the majority of the world's card transactions
Compete in digital payments; Visa's network faces competition from PayPal's peer-to-peer platform
The two largest US integrated oil and gas companies
Compete globally in oil exploration, production, and refining
Compete in vaccines, oncology, and immunology drug markets
Compete in pharmaceuticals and consumer health products
The two dominant players in the GLP-1 weight loss and diabetes drug market
The two largest US health insurers, competing across commercial, Medicare, and Medicaid markets
Compete heavily in the Medicare Advantage market
The two dominant home improvement retailers in the US
Compete in general merchandise retail; Target positions as a more upscale alternative to Walmart
Compete in retail; Costco's membership model differs from Walmart's everyday-low-price approach
Costco's warehouse model competes with Walmart's Sam's Club and retail stores
Compete for consumer dining dollars; both are global leaders in their restaurant categories
Compete in the fast-casual and premium quick-service restaurant market
Compete in aerospace and defense; both are major suppliers to the US military and commercial aviation
Compete in defense contracting, fighter jets, and space systems
Top US defense contractors competing for Pentagon contracts across missiles, radar, and aviation
The two leaders in streaming entertainment, competing for subscribers and content
Compete in media, theme parks, and streaming; Comcast owns NBCUniversal
The two largest US wireless carriers, competing for subscribers and 5G coverage
Compete in wireless services; T-Mobile has been gaining market share from AT&T and Verizon
The two leading ride-hailing platforms in the US, competing for drivers and riders
Compete in food delivery; Uber Eats and DoorDash are the two largest platforms in the US
Salesforce disrupted Oracle's enterprise software dominance; Benioff is a former Oracle executive
Compete in enterprise marketing and experience software; overlap in digital experience platforms
ServiceNow and Salesforce compete in enterprise workflow and automation platforms
Leading cybersecurity companies competing in endpoint protection, cloud security, and threat detection
Compete in network security and firewall markets
Compete in zero-trust security and secure access service edge (SASE)
CrowdStrike and SentinelOne compete directly in AI-powered endpoint security
The two largest semiconductor equipment manufacturers, supplying chip fabrication tools
Compete in process control and yield management equipment for semiconductor manufacturing
Both supply critical equipment to chip fabs; Lam focuses on etch/deposition, KLA on inspection
ASML's EUV lithography machines are essential for TSMC's most advanced chip manufacturing
Intel relies on ASML's lithography equipment for its advanced chip fabrication processes
Intel is trying to compete with TSMC in the foundry business with Intel Foundry Services
AMD relies on TSMC to manufacture its Ryzen, EPYC, and Radeon chips
Competing in AI processors for mobile and edge devices; Qualcomm's NPU vs NVIDIA's Jetson platform
Shell and ExxonMobil are global integrated energy competitors across all segments
SoFi challenges traditional banks with digital-first banking, lending, and investing
